communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) Any and all emails and Microsoft Teams messages sent or received between March 1, 2026, and the time this request is processed, between any Judge (or their judicial staff) and the Court's Information Technology (IT) department containing the word 'Dispatch'.
2) Any and all emails and Microsoft Teams messages sent or received between March 1, 2026, and the time this request is processed, between any Judge (or their judicial staff) and the Court's Information Technology (IT) department about or related to any non-disclosure agreements. 
3) Copies of agendas, meeting minutes, and meeting notes from any judges' meetings or administrative meetings held between March 1, 2026, and the date this request is processed.
4) Copies of agendas, meeting minutes, and meeting notes from any administrative meetings held between March 1, 2026, and the date this request is processed.
5) The complete administrative human resources and personnel files (including but not limited to hiring documents, disciplinary records, and performance reviews) for Teneica Plair and Terrence Scott.
6) Any administrative, payroll, or internal disciplinary files maintained by the Court for Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, Judge Lasheyl Stroud, and Judge George W. Leach.
7) All drafts, revisions, and the final executed template of the employee Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) implemented in March 2026. Additionally, all meeting notes, internal memoranda, and emails regarding the creation and implementation of this NDA.
8)The electronic access logs and/or keycard swipe data for Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, Judge Lasheyl Stroud, and Judge George W. Leach from March 1, 2025, to the present. To preemptively resolve any 'security record' exemption concerns under Sup.R. 44 or R.C. 149.433, the requester expressly consents to the redaction of all specific timestamps (hours and minutes) and specific physical door/reader locations. The requester is seeking only the number of unredacted days on which each judge's badge was utilized to access the building for the requested time period.
9) Any invoices, billing statements, or contracts for outside legal counsel engaged by the Court to draft, review, or advise on employee Non-Disclosure Agreements during the 2026 calendar year.
10) Any emails, HR memos, or internal communications discussing any court employee's refusal to sign the March 2026 Non-Disclosure Agreement, or discussing disciplinary consequences for failing to sign the agreement.
11) Any IT helpdesk tickets or service requests submitted by, or on behalf of, any domestic court judge, between March 1, 2026, and the date this request is processed, specifically including any requests relating to email retrieval, data deletion, computer wiping, or accessing security camera footage.
12) Any and all emails, including attachments and the full, unredacted recipient lists (To, Cc, and Bcc fields) from March 1, 2026, and the present that contained the blank Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) or instructions for employees to sign the NDA.
13) All vendor invoices, purchase orders, and payment records for any construction, renovation, interior design, or remodeling services performed in the courtroom or judicial chambers assigned to Judge Lasheyl Stroud from January 1, 2023, to the present.
14) All contracts, bids, or proposals from architects, general contractors, or furniture vendors regarding the renovation, remodeling, or refurnishing of Judge Stroud's courtroom and chambers.
15) Any internal emails, memoranda, or budget requests sent by or on behalf of Judge Stroud to the Administrative Judge or the Court Administration regarding funding for the renovation or remodeling of her courtroom and chambers.
16) Any and all internal emails, Microsoft Teams messages, or administrative memoranda sent or received by the Court Director, Legal Director, HR Director, Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, and/or any domestic court judge, discussing, referencing, or attempting to ascertain the identity of the requester of this specific public records request.
17) Any calendar invitations, calendar updates, emails, or Microsoft Teams messages sent or received by Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, the Court Director, any individual Judge, or judicial staff between March 5, 2026, and the time this request is processed, containing the words 'emergency', 'urgent', 'mandatory', or 'special' in reference to the scheduling or convening of a judges' meeting, administrative meeting, or all-staff meeting.
18) Any calendar invitations, calendar updates, emails, or Microsoft Teams messages sent or received by Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, the Court Director, any individual Judge, or judicial staff between March 5, 2026, and the time this request is processed, containing the word “meeting”.
19) Any and all emails, memoranda, drafts, or formal legal opinions exchanged between the Domestic Relations Court (including any Judge, Judicial staff, the Legal Director, or the HR department) and the Franklin County Prosecutor's Office regarding the drafting, legality, or implementation of the March 2026 employee Non-Disclosure Agreement, from January 1, 2026, to the present.
20) Any IT helpdesk tickets, service requests, or communications directed to the Court's Information Technology (IT) department or Franklin County Data Center requesting an IP address trace, email header analysis, domain lookup, or any other technical identity verification regarding the email address submitting this request or the properties of the documents submitted as a part of this request
21) Any and all records, including but not limited to calendar invitations, emails, SMS/text messages, or Microsoft Teams messages, sent or received on March 26, 2026, by Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, the Court Director, or any Judge of the Domestic Relations Division, regarding the scheduling, convening, or agendas for any 'emergency,' 'special,' or 'unscheduled' meeting of the judges or court leadership held on this date and any meeting records or notes from any such meeting. 


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John Doe
